page: Vultr vcg-a16-48c-496g-128vram is a GPU-accelerated virtual server belonging to the Cloud GPU family. It features 48 vCPUs based on the x86_64 Intel Core Processor Broadwell architecture running at 2.0 GHz, configured with 48 physical cores and a shared CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 496.0 GB of system memory, providing 10.33 GB of RAM per core. Graphics acceleration is delivered by eight NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs, yielding a combined 128 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 1500 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core processing capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 73,907.77 and a Passmark CPU score of 55,603.55. Memory performance is robust, showing a cached read-only small block bandwidth of 4,709.75 GB/sec and a large block write bandwidth of 101.16 GB/sec. In database operations, the server achieved 74.78 million operations per second on Redis SET tests. Large language model inference benchmarks show top-tier prompt processing speeds, reaching 8,182.48 tokens per second with a 135M model and 610.38 tokens per second with a 7B model. Text generation speeds are also strong, registering 32.71 tokens per second on the 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers a high density of GPU resources and system memory, making it suitable for multi-stream video processing, VDI hosting, and parallelized machine learning inference. However, the shared CPU allocation and the use of older Broadwell-generation Intel processors represent a performance tradeoff for workloads heavily dependent on single-core CPU execution, as reflected in the average single-core stress-ng score of 1,781.09. This hardware profile is best suited for GPU-accelerated workloads, large-scale Redis deployments, multi-threaded data decompression, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Vultr vcg-a16-48c-496g-128vram is a GPU-accelerated virtual server designed for memory-intensive and parallel processing workloads. It is equipped with 48 vCPUs on an Intel x86_64 Broadwell architecture, 496.0 GB of RAM, and eight NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs providing a total of 128 GB of VRAM. Storage is supported by a 1500 GB NVMe SSD. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance, with a Passmark score of 55,603.55, and top-tier LLM prompt processing speeds, reaching 610.38 tokens per second on a 7B model. While the shared CPU allocation presents a tradeoff for single-threaded tasks, the high GPU and memory density makes this instance highly efficient for video transcoding, VDI, and machine learning inference.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a16-48c-496g-128vram is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 48 Intel vCPUs, 496 GB RAM, 1.5 TB NVMe storage, and eight NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs with 128 GB VRAM for parallel computing workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-a16-48c-496g-128vram is a GPU-accelerated server with 48 Intel vCPUs, 496 GB RAM, and eight NVIDIA A16 GPUs for parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-density GPU and memory-optimized virtual server designed for parallel computing, video processing, and large-scale machine learning inference.
bullet_points: Eight NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs providing 128 GB of total VRAM
48 vCPUs on x86_64 Intel Broadwell architecture with 496 GB RAM
1500 GB local NVMe SSD storage for high-speed data access
Top-tier LLM prompt processing performance on 135M and 7B models
Strong multi-core processing and high-throughput Redis SET operations
Optimized for video transcoding, VDI hosting, and parallel inference
